2606.23546.title: The Energy Consumption of Transformer Fine-Tuning: A Roofline-Inspired Scaling Model
2606.23546.paper_summary: Transformer-based models underpin modern natural language processing but incur rapidly growing computational and energy costs. As training scales in both model size and parallelism, accurately predicting energy consumption has become critical for sustainable and cost-aware system design. We present a framework for modeling the energy consumption of Transformer training on multiple GPUs. Using controlled architectural sweeps of BERT models, we relate measured energy to lightweight proxies for compute, memory traffic, and hardware efficiency. Inspired by roofline models, our approach incorporates a speedup-based hardware-efficiency factor that captures the effects of tensor parallelism and fully sharded data parallelism. We derive a scaling law model that accurately predicts training energy across heterogeneous configurations.
2606.23546.paper_summary_zh: Transformer 基於模型支撐現代自然語言處理，但卻帶來迅速增長的計算和能源成本。隨著訓練在模型大小和並行性上擴展，準確預測能源消耗對於可持續和成本意識的系統設計變得至關重要。我們提出了一個框架，用於建模在多個 GPU 上的 Transformer 訓練能源消耗。通過對 BERT 模型進行受控的架構掃描，我們將測量到的能源與計算、內存流量和硬件效率的輕量級代理相關聯。受到屋頂線模型的啟發，我們的方法納入了一個基於加速的硬件效率因子，該因子捕捉了張量並行性和完全分片數據並行性的影響。我們推導出一個縮放定律模型，準確預測異構配置下的訓練能源。
2606.23546.author: Mansour Zoubeirou a Mayaki et.al.
